channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: El Salvador President Bukele says he won't be releasing a Maryland man back to the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they have no basis for the small Central American nation to return a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack by gunmen leaves at least 40 people dead in Nigeria, the country's president says
item.description: Nigeria’s president says that at least 40 people were killed when Muslim gunmen, believed to be herders, attacked a Christian farming community in the north-central part of the country, the latest in a spate of increasingly sectarian violence in the West African country.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Yellen sees 'very worrisome' pattern in Treasurys sell-off
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen has expressed concern over the economic uncertainty caused by President Trump's tariff policies, which she believes has led to a "really, very worrisome" trend for dollar-based assets.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Britain's 'Stop Brexit Man' acquitted by judge over his musical protests outside Parliament
item.description: An anti-Brexit activist who has spent years mounting a one-man protest outside Britain’s Parliament has won a court victory against a police attempt to pull the plug on his musical activities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: Social media company Meta says that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after privacy activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New York City's mayor visits the Dominican Republic after the deadly nightclub roof collapse
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams is visiting the Dominican Republic to pay his respects to the 231 people who died last week when the roof of a legendary nightclub collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Martín Adames Alcántara, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Home to centuries of heritage, Sudan's biggest museum is looted and wrecked by a 2-year war
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Inside Sudan's biggest museum, the exhibition halls once filled with statues and relics from centuries of ancient civilizations are trashed, littered with debris. The display cases stand empty and shattered. A mummy lies exposed in an open storage box.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's Xi Jinping says there are no winners in a tariff war as he visits Southeast Asia
item.description: China’s leader Xi Jinping says no one wins in a trade war as he kicked off a diplomatic tour of Southeast Asia with a trip to Vietnam.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu And Aniruddha Ghosal, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Convalescing Pope Francis opens Holy Week with in-person greeting in St. Peter's Square
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A convalescing Pope Francis greeted the crowd in St. Peter’s Square on Palm Sunday, wishing more than 20,000 faithful a “Happy Palm Sunday, Happy Holy Week," in yet another reassuring public sign of his recovery from a life-threatening battle with double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of failing to pause strikes after US envoy leaves Moscow
item.description: Russia and Ukraine’s top diplomats on Saturday used a high-level conference in Turkey to once again trade accusations of violating a tentative U.S.-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges of negotiating an end to the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump team holds 'constructive' face-to-face nuclear talks with Iran, will meet again next weekend
item.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare face-to-face nuclear talks in Oman Saturday. The Trump administration is pushing for diplomacy backed by military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dominican club collapse death toll rises to 226 after woman pulled from rubble dies in hospital
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — A woman hospitalized after being rescued from the rubble of a roof collapse at a popular nightclub in the Dominican Republic died on Saturday, raising the death toll to 226, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's army says it will fire air force reservists who condemned the war
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel’s military said Friday it will fire air force reservists who signed an open letter that condemns the war in Gaza for mainly serving political interests while failing to bring home the hostages.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian capital braces for a mega pro-president rally as tensions surge in the Balkan country
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Chanting patriotic songs, thousands of supporters of Serbia’s populist President Aleksandar Vucic gathered in downtown Belgrade on Friday, a day ahead of what authorities expect to be a huge rally in his support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin ordered to return alleged MS-13 member mistakenly deported
item.description: The justices did not say exactly what Kilmar Abrego Garcia's release would entail or how he would return to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Ksenia Karelina reunites with family after Russian detainment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Russian-American woman freed by Moscow arrived back in the United States late Thursday as part of a prisoner swap completed as the two countries aim to repair ties. A plane carrying Ksenia Karelina landed around 11 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican drug lord convicted in killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena is freed
item.description: Mexican drug lord Ernesto "Don Neto" Fonseca Carrillo was released from custody after being convicted in the 1985 killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine says more than 150 Chinese mercenaries are fighting for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Wednesday expanded on its claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting for Russia’s invading army, saying it had gathered detailed intelligence on more than 150 mercenaries Moscow allegedly recruited through social media.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 18 dead, 120 injured in Dominican Republican nightclub rooftop collapse
item.description: 18 people died and more than 120 were injured when a roof fell at a nightclub in the capital of the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation
